steps.title: Keep present tense
steps.content: The passage defines worker cooperatives in the present tense: a cooperative is owned and operated by workers, this model stands in contrast, and profits are shared. The blank should stay in that same general-present timeframe.

steps.title: Avoid past-tense shifts
steps.content: B uses past perfect, C uses simple past, and D uses past progressive. All three shift away from the present-tense explanation of how cooperatives work. The plural subject workers needs the present-tense verb describing what they do directly from the cooperative's success. The answer is A.
